shot_function: Escalate from close combat into temple architecture interaction.
Use environment tactically as part of the exchange, not as passive background.
Bridge SHOT-02 close combat toward SHOT-04 structural damage.
body_mechanics_requirements: Footwork must show traction, slip, step recovery, pivot, and shoulder turn.
Strikes should have clear contact point and rebound.
Defenders should react through torso displacement, guard collapse, forced sidestep, or foot skid.
camera_requirements: Avoid losing who is in foreground/background.
Use environment anchor points like pillars, roofline, stair edge, eaves, and corridor opening.
Camera can track laterally, rise with rooftop movement, or follow a displacement through architecture.
Avoid excessive shake that hides contact.

likely_shot_length_range.planning_only: 4 to 5 seconds for first generation candidate

route_A_column_corridor_to_roof_edge.description: Fight moves through pillar/corridor line and ends near eaves or roof edge.
benefits: Best continuity from SHOT-02 floor-level combat.
Uses clear architectural anchors for staging.
Can escalate environment without premature destruction.
risks: Columns may hide contact if camera drifts too much.
Model may treat corridor and pillars as background only unless footwork interacts with them.

route_B_stairs_to_rooftop.description: Fight climbs wet stairs and transitions onto roof tiles.
benefits: Strong visible escalation in vertical level.
Wet stairs and roof tiles give natural slip, pivot, and recovery beats.
Sets up later SHOT-04 roof/structure damage without destroying the temple yet.
risks: Rooftop movement may become floaty wuxia if foot contact is not anchored.
Fast vertical transition may hurt continuity if the starting frame is still courtyard-level.

route_C_roof_only.description: Starts on roof tiles and uses sliding/eaves/roof edge.
benefits: Clean roof-tile interaction and strong silhouette potential.
Good preparation for later structure-break escalation.
route_C_roof_only.risk: May lose continuity from SHOT-02 if not bridged.

route_A_B_hybrid.description: Start through pillar/corridor pressure, then angle toward wet stairs/eaves without fully reaching a destructive rooftop beat.
route_A_B_hybrid.why_consider: Combines the clearest continuity of Route A with the stronger escalation promise of Route B.

continuity_requirements: Keep rainy temple visual continuity from SHOT-02.
Keep both characters in the same identity system.
Maintain martial-arts realism before mythic escalation.
Leave room for SHOT-04 to escalate structural break.

character_refs.duty: Fenshou identity reference for later package design
Shuangji full-body identity reference for later package design
Highest-priority Shuangji identity anchor for later package design

scene_refs.duty: Current locked rainy temple scene anchor

risks: model may turn environment into background only
model may overdo destruction too early
roof movement may become floaty/wuxia if not anchored by footwork
rain and particles may obscure contact

options: Ask human to pick Route A, Route B, Route C, or Route A/B hybrid.
If human wants a default, recommend Route A/B hybrid: corridor/pillar pressure into wet stairs or eaves transition.
K98 can create a prompt/manifest package only after a route is selected.
Do not submit without explicit authorization.
